localBlueprintPrecedence: The local booking, waitlist, continuity, and contact-repair blueprints outrank external references if there is any conflict.

borrowed: Verb-first button labels in sentence case
Specific labels for the one next safe action
rejected: Generic Continue wording when the action can be named more precisely

borrowed: Compact summary-list structure for preference, status, and contact rows
rejected: Inline change links on every row because the waitlist view is intentionally lightweight

borrowed: Short what-happens-next copy after a completed join or accepted-pending state
rejected: A full green success page because accepted waitlist offers are not yet authoritative booking success

borrowed: Brief high-consequence wording for fallback-due posture
rejected: A separate interruption page because the waitlist continuation must stay in the same shell

borrowed: Short sentences, plain English, and factual tone
rejected: Softened reassurance when the truth chain is still pending or blocked

borrowed: One focused action in lightweight empty or no-offer states
Contextual copy instead of generic no-data filler
rejected: Illustration-heavy empty states that would make the waitlist feel like onboarding

borrowed: Dialog restraint and action-specific labels
rejected: Using a modal for join or contact repair because the patient still needs the surrounding request context

borrowed: Disabled posture only when an action is temporarily unavailable
rejected: Hiding informative blocked content from assistive technologies

borrowed: Read-only provenance cards for expired or superseded offers
rejected: Turning blocked actions into invisible states instead of visible read-only context

borrowed: Focus return and semantic guidance for any future modal need
rejected: Modal takeover for the primary waitlist flow

borrowed: Alert-dialog semantics were reviewed for high-consequence prompts
rejected: Alert-dialog usage for expiry or fallback because those states stay inline instead of interrupting focus

borrowed: Polite live status announcements for same-page waitlist state changes
rejected: Using alerts for routine state updates that do not need to interrupt the user

borrowed: role=status on same-page waitlist announcements
rejected: Custom announcement hacks without a semantic live region

borrowed: Single-column reflow with no horizontal scrolling at narrow widths
rejected: Wide two-column persistence on small screens

borrowed: ARIA snapshot coverage for normal, expired, and fallback states
rejected: Purely visual verification without accessible-tree coverage

borrowed: Accessibility-focused route checks alongside manual assertions
rejected: Treating automated accessibility checks as the only proof source

borrowed: Stable desktop and mobile screenshot baselines
Trace capture for interactive waitlist flows
rejected: Running stateful flows without debuggable traces
